parameters.tooltip: Depth to show (-d)
Number of threads to use (-T)
Specify a config file to use
Number of lines of output to show (-n)
Subdirectories will not have their path shortened (-p)
Exclude any file or directory with this path (-X)
Exclude any file or directory with a regex matching that listed in this file (-I)
dereference sym links - Treat sym links as directories and go into them (-L)
Only count the files and directories on the same filesystem as the supplied directory (-x)
Use file length instead of blocks (-s)
Print tree upside down (biggest highest) (-r)
No colors will be printed (Useful for commands like: watch) (-c)
Force colors print (-C)
No percent bars or percentages will be displayed (-b)
percent bars moved to right side of screen (-B)
Minimum size file to include in output (-z)
For screen readers. Removes bars. Adds new column: depth level (-R)
No total row will be displayed
Directory 'size' is number of child files instead of disk size (-f)
Do not display hidden files (-i)
Exclude filepaths matching this regex (-v)
Only include filepaths matching this regex (-e)
show only these file types (-t)
Specify width of output overriding the auto detection of terminal width (-w)
Disable the progress indication (-P)
Print path with errors
Only directories will be displayed (-D)
Only files will be displayed. (Finds your largest files) (-F)
Changes output display size (-o)
Specify memory to use as stack size (-S)
Output the directory tree as json to the current directory (-j)
Matches files modified more/less than n days ago (-M)
just like -mtime, but based on file access time (-A)
just like -mtime, but based on file change time (-y)
run dust on NUL-terminated file names specified in file
Keep these directories collapsed
Directory 'size' is max filetime of child files instead of disk size (-m)
Print help (-h)
Print version (-V)
